prediction.summary: A critical account takeover incident was detected for user sarah.chen. The attacker successfully authenticated from Singapore (198.18.50.22) just two minutes after a legitimate login from London, triggering an impossible travel alert and an MFA bypass anomaly. Post-compromise, the attacker created malicious email forwarding rules, connected to the VPN, and gained SSH access to a bastion host. Automated response initiated a password reset.

timeline.event: User sarah.chen successfully authenticates from London (203.0.113.88).
MFA bypass requested for sarah.chen from London (203.0.113.88).
User sarah.chen successfully authenticates from Singapore (198.18.50.22) on a new device.
Impossible travel alert triggered for sarah.chen (London to Singapore in 2 minutes).
Malicious inbox rule created by sarah.chen to forward all emails to attacker@dropmail.cc.
Malicious inbox rule created by sarah.chen to delete security alerts.
User sarah.chen connects to VPN from 198.18.50.22.
User sarah.chen gains SSH access to bastion host from 198.18.50.22.
SIEM identifies account takeover indicators: impossible travel, MFA anomaly, mail forwarding.
SIEM generates a CRITICAL alert for T1078 Valid Accounts compromise.
Automated response forces password reset for sarah.chen.
IDS reports high session hijack risk (score 92) for user sarah.chen from 198.18.50.22.

recommendations: Verify the legitimacy of the initial London login and the MFA bypass request for sarah.chen.
Confirm all active sessions for sarah.chen have been terminated and credentials reset. If not, perform these actions immediately.
Block the attacker's source IP (198.18.50.22) at the firewall or security perimeter.
Investigate all activities performed by the compromised account from 198.18.50.22, including VPN access, SSH bastion activity, and any further lateral movement.
Remove the malicious inbox rules and assess potential data exfiltration to attacker@dropmail.cc.
Review EDR/host logs on the bastion host for commands executed by sarah.chen after SSH access.
Conduct a full forensic analysis of sarah.chen's account and associated devices for any additional compromise indicators or persistence mechanisms.
